data.description:

In an interview, Paul Atkins stated, “We are looking to allow people to have access to 401(k) through professional management […] […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/sec-paul-atkins-time-right-for-crypto-401k/

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Inventory Declines More Than Expected
data.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) reported a significant decrease in natural gas inventories for the week ending January 23. According to Jin10, the inventory fell by 242 billion cubic feet, surpassing the anticipated decline of 232 billion cubic feet. The previous week's reduction was recorded at 120 billion cubic feet. This data indicates a larger-than-expected drawdown in natural gas supplies.

data.title: DePIN Market Surpasses Expectations with $10 Billion Valuation
data.description: The DePIN market has achieved an estimated valuation of $10 billion, contrary to predictions of its decline. According to NS3.AI, despite a significant drop in DePIN token prices, ranging from 94% to 99% from their peak values, major projects continue to generate consistent on-chain revenue. Last year, this revenue amounted to $72 million, highlighting a disparity between token prices and the underlying project fundamentals. This suggests that the market may be undervaluing these projects.

data.title: Long-Term Bitcoin Holders Reduce Holdings Despite Net Balance Changes
data.description:
Recent data from glassnode reveals that long-term holders (LTH) have reduced their Bitcoin holdings by over 370,000 BTC in the past 30 days, despite net position changes indicating a decrease of only 144,000 BTC. According to Foresight News, the average daily expenditure by LTHs has exceeded 12,000 BTC.

glassnode explains that the net position change reflects the net balance rather than the total amount sold. This calculation involves subtracting the amount spent by LTHs from the amount of Bitcoin transitioning from short-term holders to long-term holders. Over the past month, approximately 226,000 BTC have matured from short-term to long-term holdings, resulting in a net supply decrease of only 144,000 BTC.

The organization notes that when the maturity of coin age is high, the net indicator may underestimate the actual scale of distribution by LTHs.

data.title: Bitcoin Experiences Significant Drop Below 86000 USDT
data.description: Bitcoin has fallen below the 86000 USDT mark, currently trading at 85963.20 USDT. According to Foresight News, this represents a daily decline of 3.74%.

data.title: Spot gold and silver prices plummeted, with gold breaking through the $5400 and $5300 levels in succession.
data.description: Spot gold and silver prices continued their sharp decline. Spot gold broke through the $5,400 and $5,300 levels consecutively, before rebounding slightly to $5,327 per ounce. Spot silver fell from a high of $121.6 to $111.5 per ounce, before rebounding slightly to $113.5 per ounce. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iran: Will not initiate war; will resolutely retaliate if invaded.
data.description: On January 29 local time, Iranian First Vice President Aref, speaking about the regional situation, stated that the current government has maintained a state of readiness since taking office. Iran will not initiate war, but if war is provoked, it will defend itself with a firm stance, and "the outcome of the war will not be decided by the enemy." Aref emphasized that preparations for a state of war must be made. Iran adheres to the principle of not being the first to wage war, but if invaded, it will resolutely retaliate and ensure that the course of the war is not dictated by the enemy. He pointed out that Iran advocates dialogue and rationality, but if a conflict breaks out, its outcome will no longer be controlled by the adversary. (CCTV News)

data.title: U.S. Rare Earth and Critical Mineral Companies Experience Decline
data.description: Shares of U.S. rare earth and critical mineral companies saw a significant decline. According to Jin10, U.S. Rare Earth (USAR.O) fell by 14.5%, Mp Materials (MP.N) dropped by 9%, and Critical Metals (CRML.O) decreased by 13%. This downturn followed reports that U.S. President Donald Trump's administration decided to abandon the price floor for critical minerals.

data.title: Crypto PAC Fairshake Reported $193 Million Ahead of U.S. Midterms
data.description:
Key insights: Crypto PAC Fairshake reported holding $193 million in cash ahead of the 2026 U. S. midterm elections. The disclosure highlighted how crypto-backed political groups continued expanding their influence as regulatory debates intensified in Washington
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2026/01/29/crypto-pac-fairshake-reported-193-million-ahead-of-u-s-midterms/

data.title: Why Bitcoin’s Behavior This Week Shows It’s Being Priced Like a Risk Asset, Not a Hedge
data.description:
This week’s fluctuations in Bitcoin’s value reveal that it is being treated more like a traditional risk asset by investors rather than functioning as a hedge against market volatility, suggesting that market participants are responding to broader economic conditions and uncertainties, rather than viewing Bitcoin as a stable store of value. This behavior indicates a shifting perception in how Bitcoin is integrated into investment strategies, aligning it more closely with assets typically associated with higher risk and greater price volatility.
source: https://blocknews.com/why-bitcoins-behavior-this-week-shows-its-being-priced-like-a-risk-asset-not-a-hedge/

data.title: The crypto sector opened weakly on the US stock market, with Strategy (MSTR) falling 2.38%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.15%, the S&P 500 rose 0.19%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.12%. The crypto sector generally saw a pullback, with Bitmine down 3.41%, Strategy (MSTR) down 2.38%, SharpLink down 1.80%, Coinbase down 1.64%, Circle down 4.72%, and Solana down 1.35%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: XRP Faces Challenges in Sustaining Rallies Above $2
data.description: XRP's price struggles to maintain rallies above the $2 mark due to a substantial sell wall of approximately 1.86 billion XRP coins just below this level. According to NS3.AI, successful rallies necessitate strong buying volume and significant exchange outflows to absorb this supply, a scenario that has only materialized once in early January. While whale accumulation is taking place, it remains inadequate to counteract the persistent sell pressure. As a result, XRP remains at risk of declining if it fails to decisively break above $1.99.
